entities.context: 2539 participants with obesity, of whom 1032 also had prediabetes, were assigned in a 1:1:1:1 ratio to receive tirzepatide at a once-weekly dose of 5 mg, 10 mg, or 15 mg or placebo.

entities.name: SURMOUNT-1

attributes.primary_endpoint: percent change in body weight from baseline
key_secondary_endpoints: percent change in body weight from baseline to week 176
onset of type 2 diabetes during 176-week period
onset of type 2 diabetes during 193-week period
attributes.patient_population: adults with obesity, subgroup with both obesity and prediabetes

entities.context: We performed a phase 3, double-blind, randomized, controlled trial in which 2539 participants with obesity, of whom 1032 also had prediabetes, were assigned in a 1:1:1:1 ratio to receive tirzepatide at a once-weekly dose of 5 mg, 10 mg, or 15 mg or placebo.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor agonism
entities.context: MeSH Terms: Glucagon-Like Peptide-1 Receptor Agonists

entities.name: body weight reduction

attributes.measurement_method: percent change in body weight from baseline

entities.context: At 176 weeks, the mean percent change in body weight among the participants who received tirzepatide was -12.3% with the 5-mg dose, -18.7% with the 10-mg dose, and -19.7% with the 15-mg dose, as compared with -1.3% among those who received placebo (P<0.001 for all comparisons with placebo).

entities.name: Gastrointestinal Adverse Events
entities.context: Other than coronavirus disease 2019, the most common adverse events were gastrointestinal, most of which were mild to moderate in severity and occurred primarily during the dose-escalation period in the first 20 weeks of the trial.

entities.name: Eli Lilly
entities.context: Funded by Eli Lilly; SURMOUNT-1 ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT04184622.

entities.name: Jastreboff et al., NEJM 2025

attributes.journal: The New England Journal of Medicine

entities.context: Title: Tirzepatide for Obesity Treatment and Diabetes Prevention. Journal: The New England journal of medicine (2025). PMID: 39536238.

entities.name: prediabetes progression to T2DM biomarker

attributes.clinical_relevance: patient stratification for diabetes prevention trial

entities.context: its efficacy in reducing weight and delaying progression to type 2 diabetes in persons with both obesity and prediabetes.

relations.evidence: Three years of treatment with tirzepatide in persons with obesity and prediabetes resulted in substantial and sustained weight reduction and a markedly lower risk of progression to type 2 diabetes than that with placebo.
its efficacy in reducing weight and delaying progression to type 2 diabetes in persons with both obesity and prediabetes.
MeSH Terms: Glucagon-Like Peptide-1 Receptor Agonists
2539 participants with obesity, of whom 1032 also had prediabetes, were assigned in a 1:1:1:1 ratio to receive tirzepatide at a once-weekly dose of 5 mg, 10 mg, or 15 mg or placebo.

Other than coronavirus disease 2019, the most common adverse events were gastrointestinal, most of which were mild to moderate in severity and occurred primarily during the dose-escalation period in the first 20 weeks of the trial.
Obesity is a chronic disease and causal precursor to myriad other conditions, including type 2 diabetes.
its efficacy in reducing weight and delaying progression to type 2 diabetes in persons with both obesity and prediabetes.
Funded by Eli Lilly; SURMOUNT-1 ClinicalTrials.gov number, NCT04184622.

Title: Tirzepatide for Obesity Treatment and Diabetes Prevention. Journal: The New England journal of medicine (2025). PMID: 39536238.
its efficacy in reducing weight and delaying progression to type 2 diabetes in persons with both obesity and prediabetes.

At 176 weeks, the mean percent change in body weight among the participants who received tirzepatide was -12.3% with the 5-mg dose, -18.7% with the 10-mg dose, and -19.7% with the 15-mg dose, as compared with -1.3% among those who received placebo (P<0.001 for all comparisons with placebo).
